summary: Full E2E of NEW Password Recovery feature (forgot-password + reset-password, TEST MODE). 8 new backend tests + full UI journey (register API -> forgot on UI -> reset via link -> login with new password -> /reset-password w/o token) all PASS. Auth/Admin/Subscription regression: 34/34 PASS.

covered: forgot for existing email/password user -> found:true, token, reset_url containing /reset-password?token=; DB row inserted with used=false + expires_at
forgot for unknown email -> found:false, reset_url:null, generic message (no enumeration)
forgot for Google-only account (no password_hash) -> found:false, reset_url:null (seeded doc via pymongo)
reset with new_password shorter than 6 chars -> 400 detail mentions '6'
reset with unknown token -> 400 'Link non valido'
reset with valid token -> 200 {ok:true}, token row now used=true, bcrypt hash begins with $2b$, old password now 401, login with NEW password returns 200 + user.email

journey_covered: click forgot-password-link -> mode=forgot: password field hidden, submit button text = 'Invia link di reset'
submit known email -> reset-link-box appears with data-testid=reset-link (href contains /reset-password?token=...)
back-to-login button returns to normal login form
navigate to reset URL -> ResetPassword page shows new-password-input + confirm-password-input + reset-submit-button
mismatched passwords -> stays on form, reset-success NOT shown
matching new password -> reset-success visible; go-login-button -> /login
login with NEW password succeeds -> redirected to /dashboard
/reset-password with NO token shows 'Link non valido o mancante.' + 'Vai al login' (no form)

action_items: TEST MODE limitation is known/approved: reset_url is returned in API response and shown on screen. When email delivery is wired in, remove reset_url from response body (keep only in email) and hide reset-link-box in Auth.jsx.
OPTIONAL: forgot-password endpoint currently has no rate limiting; consider throttling per email/IP before production.
critical_code_review_comments: forgot_password correctly distinguishes email/password vs google accounts (checks user.get('password_hash')) — good; prevents password reset on OAuth-only accounts.
reset_password validates used flag AND expires_at; handles both aware and naive datetimes with a safe defensive conversion. Correct.
Backend uses secrets.token_urlsafe(32) -> ~43 char URL-safe token; sufficient entropy.
TTL index on password_reset_tokens.expires_at (line 1128 in server.py) auto-purges expired tokens — good hygiene.
ResetPassword.jsx validates length>=6 client-side AND checks confirm match before POST — matches backend rules.

FRONTEND_URL env var is read for reset_url construction; if unset it falls back to empty string ('/reset-password?token=...'). In current deployment FRONTEND_URL is set correctly. Verified reset_url starts with the correct https://... prefix.
server.py now 1156 lines — still slightly above the 700-line comfort threshold. Consider splitting soon.
test_credentials: admin@pawcare.it / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Throwaway users test_reset_*@example.com / test_reset_ui_*@example.com generated per test.
seed_data_creation: Per-test throwaway users test_reset_*/test_exp_*/test_reset_ui_* created via /api/auth/register. One temporary google-only user seeded directly into users collection and cleaned up in the same test. Real accounts (admin@pawcare.it, teresa.compi98@gmail.com) never touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Password reset flow (forgot + reset) is fully working in TEST MODE. Endpoints: POST /api/auth/forgot-password (returns reset_url in response body — intentional until email is wired), POST /api/auth/reset-password. Routes: /login (Auth.jsx), /reset-password (ResetPassword.jsx, public). Data-testids: forgot-password-link, reset-link-box, reset-link, back-to-login, new-password-input, confirm-password-input, reset-submit-button, reset-success, go-login-button. Backend suite total is now 92+ tests; run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/backend_test.py -n 0 -v.
